## [00:00 - 01:25]

What makes a successful Meta campaign overall? It's been a really interesting evolution over the last few years. And in a way, it was started because of all the iOS 14 stuff and, uh, you know, losing some tracking and attribution and whatnot. And then the kind of the explosion of AI and Metas really dove into that as well. So AI and automation. So building a campaign now lies a lot more on kind of Metas black box. I say five, eight years ago when I really kind of, you know, was building my, my brand the most for, for Facebook ads education. There's a lot of like micro targeting and all these steps to go through to make sure you get this, right? What not? But now there's so much of trusting the algorithm like metas. Hey, what do you want to accomplish? Okay, this is what I want to do. I want to get sales. And then you having to pull very few levers and then it even finds your audience for you kind of thing. That was something we were, I mean, I think people are still trying to come around to that like, Oh no, I need to select interests. I need to put in a lookalike audience or custom audience. And the latest thing now is advantage plus audience, which is basically, yes, you can give the algorithm some suggestions, but otherwise it's going to use your pixel conversion history and prior engagement with your ads. As it's starting point for how it's going to target.
